HPI: Patient has been coughing for the past 2 days along with runny nose. He vomited once last night
after finishing a bottle of milk before going to sleep. No blood in vomit. No diarrhea. Denies fever. Patient
consistently tugs on his ears and has history of recurrent ear infections.
Patient has had 4 episodes of recurrent bilateral/right otitis media:
7/15/2025: tx with Augmentin
7/26/2025: tx with Augmentin (Urgent care- thought that previous tx was with Amoxicillin, but it was
Augmentin)
10/08/2025 : tx with Amoxcillin (right AOM)
11/12/2025 (today): tx with Cefdinir

claude_vision.referral_reason: Recurrent acute suppurative otitis media (bilateral) without spontaneous rupture of tympanic membrane — referred to ENT for evaluation and consideration of PE (pressure equalization) tube placement due to 4 episodes of recurrent ear infections within approximately 4 months.
relevant_clinical_findings: 4 episodes of recurrent bilateral/right otitis media documented: 7/15/2025 (Augmentin), 7/26/2025 (Augmentin via urgent care), 10/08/2025 (Amoxicillin, right AOM), 11/12/2025 (Cefdinir, current visit)
Physical exam 11/12/2025: Bilateral TM with erythema and purulent effusion, nasal congestion, copious clear rhinorrhea, oropharynx with mild erythema
Patient consistently tugs on ears; father reports history of ear pulling
Vitals: Pulse 121, Resp 33, Temp 98.6°F, SpO2 98%, Weight 10.8 kg (23 lb 13 oz) — pediatric patient
No past medical history on file
Provider note: '4 episodes in last 6 months' (Garello, Morgan Drew, NP — 11/12/2025)
ENT visit completed 2/10/2026 with Keaton, Evelyn Legaspi, NP (New Patient/Consultation) and Hearing Evaluation/Test with Nunez, Catherine Noel, AUD — both marked Completed
Diagnosis ICD-10: H66.006 — Recurrent acute suppurative otitis media without spontaneous rupture of tympanic membrane of both sides
missing_information: No audiology/hearing test results attached — hearing evaluation was completed 2/10/2026 but no report is included in this referral packet
No ENT consultation notes from the 2/10/2026 completed visit are attached

Referring physician (Saied, Rahat, MD) is listed as the original referring provider in the reason for visit, but the referral was entered by Garello, Morgan Drew, NP — no direct contact information for either provider is included
Insurance authorization details are partially redacted/illegible (subscriber name, subscriber ID, and trace number are not clearly readable)
Nystatin on medication list (start date 9/25/2024) — no clinical context or diagnosis provided for this medication, which may indicate an undocumented condition

provider_urgency_label.label: urgent
provider_urgency_label.source: Multiple locations: (1) Referral Information section — 'Priority: Urgent'; (2) Scheduling Comments 12/03/2025 attachment description — 'URG' noted in document label 'by Herrera-Vega, Cecilia: URG'; (3) Internal message subject line — 'RE: Urgent Referral - EXPO'

criteria_check.provider_label: Urgent — marked in Priority field, attachment label ('URG'), and internal message subject ('RE: Urgent Referral - EXPO')
criteria_check.reasoning: The referring provider consistently labeled this referral as urgent across multiple locations in the document, but the clinical content (recurrent otitis media in a pediatric patient, no hearing loss, no airway compromise, no abscess, no malignancy, no foreign body) does not match any of the defined ENT urgent criteria, creating a discrepancy that requires secondary review to determine whether the urgency designation is appropriate or whether clinically relevant information was not captured in the documentation.

claude_vision.summary: This is a pediatric referral to ENT for recurrent acute suppurative otitis media (bilateral), with 4 documented episodes between July and November 2025, prompting evaluation for possible PE tube placement. The referring provider labeled the referral as urgent, and the patient has since completed an ENT new patient consultation and hearing evaluation on 2/10/2026, both marked as completed. However, no results from those completed visits are included in this packet, and the urgency designation does not clearly align with any defined ENT urgent clinical criteria, warranting secondary review.
